data.content: I need to download debug symbols for `libmonosgen-2.0.so` using its BuildId from Microsoft's symbol server, then symbolicate the addresses. Let me try.

arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; dpkg -l | grep -i llvm 2>/dev/null | head -5; which debuginfod-find 2>/dev/null

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
# Try to download the library from Microsoft symbol server using ELF BuildId convention
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
FILENAME="libmonosgen-2.0.so"

# Try several URL formats
mkdir -p /tmp/sv-182b331c0e204408934b57a7248d39af/symbols

# Format 1: elf-buildid convention
curl -sS -f -L -o /tmp/sv-182b331c0e204408934b57a7248d39af/symbols/${FILENAME} \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}" \
  -w "\nHTTP_CODE=%{http_code}\n" 2>&1 || echo "Format 1 failed"

# Format 2: debug info
curl -sS -f -L -o /tmp/sv-182b331c0e204408934b57a7248d39af/symbols/${FILENAME}.debug \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" \
  -w "\nHTTP_CODE=%{http_code}\n" 2>&1 || echo "Format 2 failed"
arguments.description: Download symbols from Microsoft symbol server

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Download symbols from Microsoft symbol server

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to symbolicate the .NET runtime frames in the tombstone. The tombstone shows frames from `libmonosgen-2.0.so` with a BuildId of `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`. These are unsymbolicated - they only show PC offsets.

To symbolicate these, I would need:
1. The debug symbols for `libmonosgen-2.0.so` with BuildId `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`


2. A tool like `addr2line` or `llvm-symbolizer` to resolve the addresses

The debug symbols for Mono runtime are published as part of .NET's symbol packages, and I can use the BuildId to look them up from Microsoft's symbol server at `https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/`.

The URL format follows the pattern of the filename, BuildId, and filename again, or I could use `debuginfod` instead. Let me work through the standard symbolicating approach: download the debug symbols using the BuildId, then use `llvm-symbolizer` or `addr2line` to map addresses to function names. The Microsoft symbol server has specific URL patterns for ELF files with the BuildId embedded in the path.

I should check what tools are available on the system first, then attempt to fetch the symbols using one of these approaches.

arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; dpkg -l | grep -i llvm 2>/dev/null | head -5; which debuginfod-find 2>/dev/null

# Try to download the library from Microsoft symbol server using ELF BuildId convention
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
FILENAME="libmonosgen-2.0.so"

# Try several URL formats
mkdir -p /tmp/sv-182b331c0e204408934b57a7248d39af/symbols

# Format 1: elf-buildid convention
curl -sS -f -L -o /tmp/sv-182b331c0e204408934b57a7248d39af/symbols/${FILENAME} \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}" \
  -w "\nHTTP_CODE=%{http_code}\n" 2>&1 || echo "Format 1 failed"

# Format 2: debug info
curl -sS -f -L -o /tmp/sv-182b331c0e204408934b57a7248d39af/symbols/${FILENAME}.debug \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" \
  -w "\nHTTP_CODE=%{http_code}\n" 2>&1 || echo "Format 2 failed"
arguments.description: Download symbols from Microsoft symbol server

toolArgs.command:
# Try to download the library from Microsoft symbol server using ELF BuildId convention
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
FILENAME="libmonosgen-2.0.so"

# Try several URL formats
mkdir -p /tmp/sv-182b331c0e204408934b57a7248d39af/symbols

# Format 1: elf-buildid convention
curl -sS -f -L -o /tmp/sv-182b331c0e204408934b57a7248d39af/symbols/${FILENAME} \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}" \
  -w "\nHTTP_CODE=%{http_code}\n" 2>&1 || echo "Format 1 failed"

# Format 2: debug info
curl -sS -f -L -o /tmp/sv-182b331c0e204408934b57a7248d39af/symbols/${FILENAME}.debug \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" \
  -w "\nHTTP_CODE=%{http_code}\n" 2>&1 || echo "Format 2 failed"
toolArgs.description: Download symbols from Microsoft symbol server
